```{r}
  # Black dots represent the median of the most significant break years
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", size = 2, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = nice_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = nice_cols) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "b)    ", x = NULL, y = "Year") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )

# 4. Assemble with Patchwork
# Note: Nature figures often use specific widths (89mm or 183mm). 
# Adjust the width/height in ggsave accordingly.
combined_plot <- p1 / p2 + 
  plot_annotation(
    caption = "Black points indicate median values. Violins show density distribution of detected structural breaks.",
    theme = theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, italic = TRUE))
  )

print(combined_plot)

# 5. Export for Publication
ggsave("Figure_Breaks_Nature.pdf", combined_plot, width = 180, height = 220, units = "mm")

combined_plot <- p1 / p2 + 
  plot_annotation(
    caption = "Black points indicate median values. Violins show density distribution of detected structural breaks.",
    theme = theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, italic = TRUE))
  )

print(combined_plot)

# Export for publication
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(datpthout, "Figure_Breaks_Nature.png"),
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 180,
  height = 220,
  units = "mm",
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)

```

## 6. Suplementary data output

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# SUPPLEMENTARY / ANNEX DATA EXPORT FOR PUBLICATION
# Generates:
#   Table S1 – Break detection summary
#   Table S2 – Regime comparison (levels, AMEs before/after)
#   Table S3 – Crop-level summary statistics
#   Table S4 – Heat-penalty direction counts
#   Annex 1 (PDF) – S1–S4 rendered from MEMORY (no CSV read-back)
# =====================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)

# Your deltas object is the regime-wide AME comparison table
test_df_3 <- deltas

out_dir <- file.path(datpthout, "Supplementary")
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ============================================================================
# TABLE S1 — Break detection summary (from break test)
# ============================================================================
table_S1_break_summary <- break_output$breaks_table %>%
  transmute(
    country = country,
    crop = crop,
    break_year = break_year,
    F_stat = F_stat,
    p_value = p_value,
    significant = significant
  ) %>%
  arrange(country, crop)

write_csv(table_S1_break_summary,
          file.path(out_dir, "Table_S1_Break_Summary.csv"))

# ============================================================================
# TABLE S2 — Regime comparison (LEVELS AMEs before vs after break)
# ============================================================================
table_S2_regime_levels <- test_df_3 %>%
  transmute(
    country = ccc,
    crop = ppp,
    AME_GDD_before  = Total_GDD_before,
    AME_GDD_after   = Total_GDD_after,
    d_GDD           = d_GDD,
    AME_Prec_before = Tot_Precip_before,
    AME_Prec_after  = Tot_Precip_after,
    d_Prec          = d_Prec,
    Trend_before    = yyy_before,
    Trend_after     = yyy_after,
    d_Trend         = d_YYY
  ) %>%
  arrange(country, crop)

write_csv(table_S2_regime_levels,
          file.path(out_dir, "Table_S2_Regime_Levels_AMEs.csv"))

# ============================================================================
# TABLE S3 — Crop-level summary (share stronger heat penalty)
# ============================================================================
table_S3_crop_summary <- test_df_3 %>%
  group_by(ppp) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs = sum(!is.na(d_GDD)),
    share_stronger_heat_penalty = mean(d_GDD < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_weaker_heat_penalty   = mean(d_GDD > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_d_GDD  = median(d_GDD, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_d_Prec = median(d_Prec, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(crop = ppp) %>%
  arrange(desc(share_stronger_heat_penalty))

write_csv(table_S3_crop_summary,
          file.path(out_dir, "Table_S3_Crop_Level_Summary.csv"))

# ============================================================================
# TABLE S4 — Direction counts (heat penalty classification)
# ============================================================================
table_S4_heat_direction <- test_df_3 %>%
  mutate(heat_response = case_when(
    is.na(d_GDD) ~ NA_character_,
    d_GDD < 0 ~ "Stronger heat penalty",
    d_GDD > 0 ~ "Weaker heat penalty",
    TRUE ~ "No change"
  )) %>%
  count(ppp, heat_response, name = "n") %>%
  rename(crop = ppp)

write_csv(table_S4_heat_direction,
          file.path(out_dir, "Table_S4_Heat_Penalty_Direction.csv"))

# ============================================================================
# ANNEX 1 (PDF): Supplementary break data tables — FROM MEMORY
# ============================================================================

stopifnot(
  exists("table_S1_break_summary"),
  exists("table_S2_regime_levels"),
  exists("table_S3_crop_summary"),
  exists("table_S4_heat_direction")
)

# ---- helper: render a (possibly long) df across multiple PDF pages ----
df_to_pages <- function(df, title, note = NULL,
                        max_rows = 40, digits = 4, font = 7) {

  df_print <- df
  num_cols <- vapply(df_print, is.numeric, logical(1))
  df_print[num_cols] <- lapply(df_print[num_cols], function(x) round(x, digits))

  n <- nrow(df_print)
  if (n == 0) {
    grid.newpage()
    grid.text(title, x = 0.02, y = 0.98, just = c("left","top"),
              gp = gpar(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 12))
    grid.text("(No rows)", x = 0.02, y = 0.92, just = c("left","top"),
              gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, col = "grey35"))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  starts <- seq(1, n, by = max_rows)

  for (k in seq_along(starts)) {
    i1 <- starts[k]
    i2 <- min(i1 + max_rows - 1, n)
    chunk <- df_print[i1:i2, , drop = FALSE]

    tg <- tableGrob(
      chunk, rows = NULL,
      theme = ttheme_minimal(
        core   = list(fg_params = list(cex = font/10)),
        colhead= list(fg_params = list(cex = (font+1)/10, fontface = "bold"))
      )
    )

    grid.newpage()
    grid.text(
      if (length(starts) > 1) paste0(title, " (rows ", i1, "–", i2, " of ", n, ")") else title,
      x = 0.02, y = 0.98, just = c("left","top"),
      gp = gpar(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 12)
    )

    if (!is.null(note)) {
      grid.text(note, x = 0.02, y = 0.945, just = c("left","top"),
                gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, col = "grey25"))
    }

    # generous viewport; table will paginate rather than clip horizontally
    pushViewport(viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.46, width = 0.98, height = 0.86))
    grid.draw(tg)
    popViewport()
  }

  invisible(NULL)
}

# ---- output file ----
annex_file <- file.path(out_dir, "Annex_1_Supplementary_Break_Tables.pdf")
pdf(annex_file, width = 11, height = 8.5)  # landscape

# ---- cover page ----
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Annex 1 — Supplementary Break Data Tables",
          x = 0.5, y = 0.62, gp = gpar(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 18))
grid.text(paste("Generated:", Sys.Date()),
          x = 0.5, y = 0.54, gp = gpar(fontsize = 11))
grid.text("Tables S1–S4 generated directly from the replication pipeline.",
          x = 0.5, y = 0.42, gp = gpar(fontsize = 10))

# ---- S1 ----
df_to_pages(
  table_S1_break_summary,
  "Table S1 — Break detection summary",
  "Candidate breaks with F-statistic, p-value, and significance flag.",
  max_rows = 45, font = 7
)

# ---- S2 (SPLIT WIDE TABLE INTO TWO PAGES BY COLUMNS) ----
S2a <- table_S2_regime_levels %>%
  dplyr::select(country, crop, AME_GDD_before, AME_GDD_after, d_GDD, Trend_before, Trend_after, d_Trend)

S2b <- table_S2_regime_levels %>%
  dplyr::select(country, crop, AME_Prec_before, AME_Prec_after, d_Prec, Trend_before, Trend_after, d_Trend)

df_to_pages(
  S2a,
  "Table S2a — Regime comparison (levels): GDD",
  "Average marginal effects before/after break; levels-based comparison.",
  max_rows = 45, font = 7
)

df_to_pages(
  S2b,
  "Table S2b — Regime comparison (levels): Precipitation",
  "Average marginal effects before/after break; levels-based comparison.",
  max_rows = 45, font = 7
)

# ---- S3 ----
df_to_pages(
  table_S3_crop_summary,
  "Table S3 — Crop-level heat penalty summary",
  "Share of series with stronger post-break heat penalty; median ΔGDD and ΔPrecip.",
  max_rows = 60, font = 7
)

# ---- S4 ----
df_to_pages(
  table_S4_heat_direction,
  "Table S4 — Heat penalty direction counts",
  "Counts of stronger / weaker / unchanged post-break heat penalty by crop.",
  max_rows = 60, font = 7
)

dev.off()

cat("✅ Supplementary tables exported to:", out_dir, "\n")
cat("✅ Annex 1 PDF generated:", annex_file, "\n")
```

### --- EXTRA SANITY CHECKS ---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

pstars <- function(p) dplyr::case_when(
  is.na(p) ~ "",
  p < 0.01 ~ "***",
  p < 0.05 ~ "**",
  p < 0.1  ~ "*",
  TRUE ~ ""
)

get_term <- function(tidy_df, term_name) {
  out <- tidy_df %>% filter(term == term_name)
  if (nrow(out) == 0) return(tibble(estimate = NA_real_, std.error = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
  out %>% select(estimate, std.error, p.value)
}


target_country <- "FRA"
france_crops   <- c("BA","MA","RP","WT","SB")

# dominant break per crop (Chow-significant highest F)
fr_breaks <- break_output$breaks_table %>%
  filter(country == target_country, crop %in% france_crops, significant) %>%
  group_by(crop) %>%
  slice_max(F_stat, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(crop, break_year)

# keep all crops; those w/o break will be NA
fr_breaks <- tibble(crop = france_crops) %>%
  left_join(fr_breaks, by = "crop")

fr_ts <- yield_climate_data_cleaned %>%
  filter(ccc == target_country, ppp %in% france_crops) %>%
  mutate(ppp = as.character(ppp))

fit_crop_interaction <- function(crop_code, byear) {
  
  df <- fr_ts %>% filter(ppp == crop_code) %>% arrange(yyy)
  
  if (is.na(byear)) {
    # no break: fit baseline model only (no Δ)
    mod <- lm(YLD ~ Total_GDD + I(Total_GDD^2) + Tot_Precip + I(Tot_Precip^2) + yyy, data = df)
    td  <- broom::tidy(mod)
    
    gdd_b  <- get_term(td, "Total_GDD")
    prc_b  <- get_term(td, "Tot_Precip")
    
    return(tibble(
      Crop = crop_code, Break = NA_integer_,
      me_gdd_before = gdd_b$estimate,
      me_gdd_after  = NA_real_,
      d_me_gdd      = NA_real_, p_d_me_gdd = NA_real_, lo_d_me_gdd = NA_real_, hi_d_me_gdd = NA_real_,
      me_prec_before = prc_b$estimate,
      me_prec_after  = NA_real_,
      d_me_prec      = NA_real_, p_d_me_prec = NA_real_, lo_d_me_prec = NA_real_, hi_d_me_prec = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  df <- df %>% mutate(post = as.integer(yyy >= byear))
  
  mod <- lm(
    YLD ~ (Total_GDD + I(Total_GDD^2) + Tot_Precip + I(Tot_Precip^2) + yyy) * post,
    data = df
  )
  
  td <- broom::tidy(mod)
  
  # BEFORE slopes
  gdd_b <- get_term(td, "Total_GDD")
  prc_b <- get_term(td, "Tot_Precip")
  
  # DELTAS are interaction slopes (After - Before)
  gdd_d <- get_term(td, "Total_GDD:post")
  prc_d <- get_term(td, "Tot_Precip:post")
  
  # 95% CI for deltas
  gdd_lo <- gdd_d$estimate - 1.96 * gdd_d$std.error
  gdd_hi <- gdd_d$estimate + 1.96 * gdd_d$std.error
  
  prc_lo <- prc_d$estimate - 1.96 * prc_d$std.error
  prc_hi <- prc_d$estimate + 1.96 * prc_d$std.error
  
  tibble(
    Crop = crop_code, Break = as.integer(byear),
    
    me_gdd_before = gdd_b$estimate,
    me_gdd_after  = gdd_b$estimate + gdd_d$estimate,
    d_me_gdd      = gdd_d$estimate,
    p_d_me_gdd    = gdd_d$p.value,
    lo_d_me_gdd   = gdd_lo,
    hi_d_me_gdd   = gdd_hi,
    
    me_prec_before = prc_b$estimate,
    me_prec_after  = prc_b$estimate + prc_d$estimate,
    d_me_prec      = prc_d$estimate,
    p_d_me_prec    = prc_d$p.value,
    lo_d_me_prec   = prc_lo,
    hi_d_me_prec   = prc_hi
  )
}

fr_table_raw <- fr_breaks %>%
  mutate(tab = map2(crop, break_year, fit_crop_interaction)) %>%
  select(tab) %>%
  unnest(tab)



fr_table_show <- fr_table_raw %>%
  transmute(
    Crop,
    Break = ifelse(is.na(Break), "—", as.character(Break)),
    
    `ME(GDD) Before`  = signif(me_gdd_before, 3),
    `ME(GDD) After`   = signif(me_gdd_after, 3),
    `ΔME(GDD)`        = ifelse(is.na(d_me_gdd), "NA",
                               paste0(signif(d_me_gdd, 3), pstars(p_d_me_gdd))),
    
    `ME(Prec) Before` = signif(me_prec_before, 3),
    `ME(Prec) After`  = signif(me_prec_after, 3),
    `ΔME(Prec)`       = ifelse(is.na(d_me_prec), "NA",
                               paste0(signif(d_me_prec, 3), pstars(p_d_me_prec)))
  )

fr_table_show


library(flextable)

ft <- flextable(fr_table_show) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  fontsize(size = 9, part = "all") %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "all")

ft



delta_long <- fr_table_raw %>%
  select(Crop, Break,
         d_me_gdd, lo_d_me_gdd, hi_d_me_gdd, p_d_me_gdd,
         d_me_prec, lo_d_me_prec, hi_d_me_prec, p_d_me_prec) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(Crop, Break),
    names_to = c(".value","var"),
    names_pattern = "(d_me|lo_d_me|hi_d_me|p_d_me)_(gdd|prec)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    variable = recode(var, gdd = "ΔME(GDD)", prec = "ΔME(Precip)"),
    sig_cat = case_when(
      p_d_me < 0.05 ~ "p<0.05",
      p_d_me < 0.1  ~ "p<0.10",
      TRUE ~ "n.s."
    ),
    Crop = factor(Crop, levels = france_crops)
  )

p_delta <- ggplot(delta_long, aes(x = d_me, y = Crop)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo_d_me, xmax = hi_d_me, shape = sig_cat),
                  linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "France: regime shift in climate sensitivity at dominant breakpoints",
    subtitle = "Δ marginal effects (After − Before); 95% CI from interaction term",
    x = "Δ marginal effect (After − Before)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_delta)

ggsave(file.path(datpthout, "Fig_France_DeltaME_Dotplots.pdf"),
       p_delta, width = 180, height = 95, units = "mm", device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave(file.path(datpthout, "Fig_France_DeltaME_Dotplots.png"),
       p_delta, width = 180, height = 95, units = "mm", dpi = 600)





```

#sanity With and without SUPf

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPF vs NO-SUPF COMPARISON WORKFLOW (FULL DIAGNOSTIC BLOCK)
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)

# -------------------------------
# 1. Run both specifications
# -------------------------------
out_nosupf <- run_break_analysis(
  data = yield_climate_data_cleaned,
  selected_crops = selected_crops,
  selected_countries = selected_countries,
  model_formula = "YLD ~ Total_GDD + I(Total_GDD^2) + Tot_Precip + I(Tot_Precip^2) + yyy",
  window_years = 15,
  global_test = FALSE
)

out_supf <- run_break_analysis(
  data = yield_climate_data_cleaned,
  selected_crops = selected_crops,
  selected_countries = selected_countries,
  model_formula = "YLD ~ Total_GDD + I(Total_GDD^2) + Tot_Precip + I(Tot_Precip^2) + yyy",
  window_years = 15,
  global_test = TRUE
)

# -------------------------------
# 2. Panel coverage comparison
# -------------------------------
nosupf_pairs <- out_nosupf$breaks_summary %>% select(country, crop)
supf_pairs   <- out_supf$breaks_summary   %>% select(country, crop)

filtered_out <- anti_join(nosupf_pairs, supf_pairs, by = c("country","crop"))
common_pairs <- inner_join(nosupf_pairs, supf_pairs, by = c("country","crop"))

coverage_summary <- tibble(
  total_panels_nosupf = nrow(nosupf_pairs),
  total_panels_supf   = nrow(supf_pairs),
  panels_removed_by_supf = nrow(filtered_out),
  share_removed_pct = round(100 * nrow(filtered_out) / nrow(nosupf_pairs), 1)
)

# -------------------------------
# 3. Compare dominant break years
# -------------------------------
cmp <- inner_join(
  out_nosupf$breaks_summary %>%
    select(country, crop,
           break_year_nosupf = year_max_F,
           chow_p_nosupf = chow_p_value,
           global_p_nosupf = global_p_value,
           supF_year_nosupf = supF_year),
  out_supf$breaks_summary %>%
    select(country, crop,
           break_year_supf = year_max_F,
           chow_p_supf = chow_p_value,
           global_p_supf = global_p_value,
           supF_year_supf = supF_year),
  by = c("country","crop")
)

break_year_change_summary <- tibble(
  panels_compared = nrow(cmp),
  break_year_changed = sum(cmp$break_year_nosupf != cmp$break_year_supf, na.rm = TRUE)
)

# -------------------------------
# 4. Classify break strength (Chow)
# -------------------------------
classify_strength <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "NA",
    p <= 0.01 ~ "Strong",
    p <= 0.05 ~ "Moderate",
    p <= 0.10 ~ "Weak",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  )
}

break_strength_table <- out_nosupf$breaks_summary %>%
  mutate(
    chow_strength = classify_strength(chow_p_value),
    chow_sig_05 = !is.na(chow_p_value) & chow_p_value <= 0.05
  ) %>%
  count(chow_strength, name = "n_panels")

# -------------------------------
# 5. Global vs Local support flags
# -------------------------------
support_flags_table <- out_nosupf$breaks_summary %>%
  mutate(
    global_supported = !is.na(global_p_value) & global_p_value <= 0.05,
    local_supported  = !is.na(chow_p_value) & chow_p_value <= 0.05,
    support_class = case_when(
      global_supported & local_supported ~ "Global + Local",
      global_supported & !local_supported ~ "Global only",
      !global_supported & local_supported ~ "Local only",
      TRUE ~ "Neither"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(support_class, name = "n_panels")

# -------------------------------
# 6. Diagnostic disagreement table
# -------------------------------
diagnostic_table <- out_nosupf$breaks_summary %>%
  mutate(
    global_supported = !is.na(global_p_value) & global_p_value <= 0.05,
    local_supported  = !is.na(chow_p_value) & chow_p_value <= 0.05,
    support_class = case_when(
      global_supported & local_supported ~ "Global + Local",
      global_supported & !local_supported ~ "Global only",
      !global_supported & local_supported ~ "Local only",
      TRUE ~ "Neither"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    country, crop,
    supF_year, supF_stat, global_p_value,
    year_max_F, max_F_stat, chow_p_value,
    support_class
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(support_class), chow_p_value)

disagreement_only <- diagnostic_table %>%
  filter(support_class %in% c("Global only", "Local only"))

# -------------------------------
# 7. Print compact summary
# -------------------------------
cat("\n==============================\n")
cat("SUPF vs NO-SUPF COMPARISON\n")
cat("==============================\n")

cat("\nPanel Coverage:\n")
print(coverage_summary)

cat("\nBreak-Year Differences:\n")
print(break_year_change_summary)

cat("\nBreak Strength Distribution (Chow):\n")
print(break_strength_table)

cat("\nGlobal vs Local Support:\n")
print(support_flags_table)

cat("\nPanels Removed by supF (first 20):\n")
print(head(filtered_out, 20))

cat("\nDisagreement Table (first 20 rows):\n")
print(head(disagreement_only, 20))

# Optional: return all objects for further use
comparison_results <- list(
  coverage_summary = coverage_summary,
  break_year_change_summary = break_year_change_summary,
  break_strength_table = break_strength_table,
  support_flags_table = support_flags_table,
  filtered_out_panels = filtered_out,
  diagnostic_table = diagnostic_table,
  disagreement_only = disagreement_only
)

```

# Expanding on the sanity

```{r}

# ============================================================
# BREAK QUALITY & ROBUSTNESS ANALYSIS
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

df <- out_nosupf$breaks_summary

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Add strength + support classification
# ------------------------------------------------------------
df2 <- df %>%
  mutate(
    chow_strength = case_when(
      is.na(chow_p_value) ~ "NA",
      chow_p_value <= 0.01 ~ "Strong",
      chow_p_value <= 0.05 ~ "Moderate",
      chow_p_value <= 0.10 ~ "Weak",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"
    ),
    global_supported = !is.na(global_p_value) & global_p_value <= 0.05,
    local_supported  = !is.na(chow_p_value) & chow_p_value <= 0.05,
    support_class = case_when(
      global_supported & local_supported ~ "Global + Local",
      global_supported & !local_supported ~ "Global only",
      !global_supported & local_supported ~ "Local only",
      TRUE ~ "Neither"
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. TABLE: Global+Local vs others by crop
# ------------------------------------------------------------
table_support_crop <- df2 %>%
  mutate(class2 = if_else(support_class == "Global + Local", "Global+Local", "Other")) %>%
  count(crop, class2) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = class2, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(
    total = `Global+Local` + Other,
    share_global_local = round(100 * `Global+Local` / total, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(share_global_local))

table_support_crop
# ---- This is your paper-ready table ----

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FIGURE: Share of Strong vs Weak breaks by crop
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fig_strength <- df2 %>%
  filter(chow_strength %in% c("Strong","Moderate","Weak")) %>%
  mutate(group = if_else(chow_strength == "Strong", "Strong", "Weak/Moderate")) %>%
  count(crop, group) %>%
  group_by(crop) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

p_strength <- ggplot(fig_strength, aes(x = crop, y = share, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Strong" = "#1b9e77", "Weak/Moderate" = "#d95f02")) +
  labs(
    title = "Strength of detected structural breaks by crop",
    x = "Crop",
    y = "Share of breaks",
    fill = "Break strength"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

print(p_strength)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. BREAK CREDIBILITY INDEX
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Combines:
#   - Chow F-stat magnitude
#   - Chow p-value
#   - Global support
#
# Normalization makes index comparable across panels.

df_index <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    F_norm = max_F_stat / max(max_F_stat, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_norm = 1 - pmin(chow_p_value, 0.10) / 0.10,
    support_bonus = case_when(
      support_class == "Global + Local" ~ 1,
      support_class == "Local only" ~ 0.7,
      support_class == "Global only" ~ 0.5,
      TRUE ~ 0.2
    ),
    credibility_index = round(100 * (0.5 * F_norm + 0.3 * p_norm + 0.2 * support_bonus), 1)
  )

# Summary distribution
credibility_summary <- df_index %>%
  summarise(
    mean_index = mean(credibility_index, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_index = median(credibility_index, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_index = min(credibility_index, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_index = max(credibility_index, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

credibility_summary

# Top and bottom examples
top_breaks <- df_index %>%
  arrange(desc(credibility_index)) %>%
  select(country, crop, year_max_F, credibility_index) %>%
  head(10)

low_breaks <- df_index %>%
  arrange(credibility_index) %>%
  select(country, crop, year_max_F, credibility_index) %>%
  head(10)

top_breaks
low_breaks

# Optional: histogram
ggplot(df_index, aes(x = credibility_index)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, fill = "#2c7fb8", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Break Credibility Index",
    x = "Credibility index (0–100)",
    y = "Number of panels"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# FRANCE MAIZE (MA) LEVELS DIAGNOSTIC PLOT
# - observed yields
# - fitted values before and after the dominant break
# - break year and segment R² shown on the figure
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------
# 1. Settings
# ----------------------------
target_country <- "FRA"
target_crop    <- "MA"

base_formula <- YLD ~ Total_GDD + I(Total_GDD^2) + Tot_Precip + I(Tot_Precip^2) + yyy

# ----------------------------
# 2. Get dominant break year
# ----------------------------
fr_ma_break <- break_output$breaks_table %>%
  filter(country == target_country, crop == target_crop, significant) %>%
  slice_max(F_stat, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

stopifnot(nrow(fr_ma_break) == 1)

break_year <- fr_ma_break$break_year[[1]]

# ----------------------------
# 3. Prepare data
# ----------------------------
df_ma <- yield_climate_data_cleaned %>%
  filter(ccc == target_country, ppp == target_crop) %>%
  arrange(yyy) %>%
  mutate(regime = if_else(yyy < break_year, "Before", "After"))

stopifnot(nrow(df_ma) > 0)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Fit separate levels models
# ----------------------------
df_before <- df_ma %>% filter(yyy < break_year)
df_after  <- df_ma %>% filter(yyy >= break_year)

mod_before <- lm(base_formula, data = df_before)
mod_after  <- lm(base_formula, data = df_after)

df_before <- df_before %>%
  mutate(fitted = predict(mod_before, newdata = df_before))

df_after <- df_after %>%
  mutate(fitted = predict(mod_after, newdata = df_after))

plot_df <- bind_rows(df_before, df_after)

r2_before <- summary(mod_before)$r.squared
r2_after  <- summary(mod_after)$r.squared

# ----------------------------
# 5. Label positions
# ----------------------------
y_top <- max(plot_df$YLD, plot_df$fitted, na.rm = TRUE)
y_bot <- min(plot_df$YLD, plot_df$fitted, na.rm = TRUE)
y_rng <- y_top - y_bot

ann_df <- tibble(
  x = c(min(df_before$yyy, na.rm = TRUE) + 2,
        max(df_after$yyy, na.rm = TRUE) - 2),
  y = c(y_top - 0.08 * y_rng,
        y_top - 0.50 * y_rng),
  lab = c(
    paste0("Before break\nR² = ", sprintf("%.3f", r2_before)),
    paste0("After break\nR² = ", sprintf("%.3f", r2_after))
  ),
  hjust = c(0, 1)
)

# ----------------------------
# 6. Plot
# ----------------------------
p_fr_ma <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = yyy)) +
  # observed series
  geom_line(aes(y = YLD), linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey35") +
  geom_point(aes(y = YLD), size = 1.6, color = "grey20") +

  # fitted values by regime
  geom_line(
    data = df_before,
    aes(y = fitted),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    color = "#1f78b4"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = df_after,
    aes(y = fitted),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    color = "#d95f02"
  ) +

  # break line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = break_year,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.7,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # break year label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = break_year + 0.5,
    y = y_top - 0.02 * y_rng,
    label = as.character(break_year),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 4
  ) +

  # R2 annotations
  geom_label(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = lab, hjust = hjust),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.4,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "white"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = pretty(plot_df$yyy, n = 6),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.06))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "France maize: levels-based structural break diagnostic",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Observed yields with regime-specific fitted values; dominant break year = ",
      break_year
    ),
    x = "Year",
    y = "Yield (t/ha)",
    caption = "Grey = observed yields; blue = pre-break fitted values; orange = post-break fitted values."
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_fr_ma)

# ----------------------------
# 7. Save high-res PNG
# ----------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(datpthout, "France_Maize_Levels_Break_Diagnostic.png"),
  plot = p_fr_ma,
  width = 180,
  height = 110,
  units = "mm",
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)
```

```{r}
plot_france_levels_break <- function(crop_code) {
  br <- break_output$breaks_table %>%
    filter(country == "FRA", crop == crop_code, significant) %>%
    slice_max(F_stat, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

  if (nrow(br) == 0) return(NULL)

  break_year <- br$break_year[[1]]

  df_crop <- yield_climate_data_cleaned %>%
    filter(ccc == "FRA", ppp == crop_code) %>%
    arrange(yyy)

  df_before <- df_crop %>% filter(yyy < break_year)
  df_after  <- df_crop %>% filter(yyy >= break_year)

  if (nrow(df_before) < 10 || nrow(df_after) < 10) return(NULL)

  mod_before <- lm(base_formula, data = df_before)
  mod_after  <- lm(base_formula, data = df_after)

  df_before <- df_before %>% mutate(fitted = predict(mod_before, newdata = df_before))
  df_after  <- df_after  %>% mutate(fitted = predict(mod_after,  newdata = df_after))

  plot_df <- bind_rows(df_before, df_after)

  r2_before <- summary(mod_before)$r.squared
  r2_after  <- summary(mod_after)$r.squared

  y_top <- max(plot_df$YLD, plot_df$fitted, na.rm = TRUE)
  y_bot <- min(plot_df$YLD, plot_df$fitted, na.rm = TRUE)
  y_rng <- y_top - y_bot

  ann_df <- tibble(
    x = c(min(df_before$yyy, na.rm = TRUE) + 2,
          max(df_after$yyy, na.rm = TRUE) - 2),
    y = c(y_top - 0.08 * y_rng,
          y_top - 0.50 * y_rng),
    lab = c(
      paste0("Before break\nR² = ", sprintf("%.3f", r2_before)),
      paste0("After break\nR² = ", sprintf("%.3f", r2_after))
    ),
    hjust = c(0, 1)
  )

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = yyy)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = YLD), linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey35") +
    geom_point(aes(y = YLD), size = 1.6, color = "grey20") +
    geom_line(data = df_before, aes(y = fitted), linewidth = 1.2, color = "#1f78b4") +
    geom_line(data = df_after,  aes(y = fitted), linewidth = 1.2, color = "#d95f02") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = break_year, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = break_year + 0.5,
      y = y_top - 0.02 * y_rng,
      label = as.character(break_year),
      hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4
    ) +
    geom_label(
      data = ann_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = lab, hjust = hjust),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      size = 3.4,
      linewidth = 0.2,
      fill = "white"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = pretty(plot_df$yyy, n = 6),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.06))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("France ", crop_code, ": levels-based structural break diagnostic"),
      subtitle = paste0("Observed yields with regime-specific fitted values; dominant break year = ", break_year),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Yield (t/ha)"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}
france_break_plot_crops <- fr_breaks %>%
  filter(has_break) %>%
  pull(crop)

france_break_plots <- purrr::set_names(
  purrr::map(france_break_plot_crops, plot_france_levels_break),
  france_break_plot_crops
)

purrr::iwalk(france_break_plots, ~ {
  if (!is.null(.x)) {
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(datpthout, paste0("France_", .y, "_Levels_Break_Diagnostic.png")),
      plot = .x,
      width = 180, height = 110, units = "mm", dpi = 600, bg = "white"
    )
  }
})
```
